sections.content: Let me check what changed after clicking blue at cols 46-47.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']

print("BEFORE white regions:")
for n in before:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  px={n['pixels']}, r={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, c={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

print("\nAFTER white regions:")
for n in after:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  px={n['pixels']}, r={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, c={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

print("\nAFTER colored markers:")
for n in after:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'N', 'p', 'Y'] and n['pixels'] < 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, r={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, c={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After clicking blue at cols 46-47:
- White at cols 50-57: px 220→204, r=30-57→32-57 (shrunk from top by 2 rows)
- White at cols 38-47: px 52→72, r=52-57→50-57 (expanded up by 2 rows)
- Colored markers at cols 52-54: charcoal 27-28→29-30, yellow 29→31 (moved DOWN by 2)
- Colored markers at cols 41-43: charcoal 49-50→47-48, purple 51→49 (moved UP by 2)
So clicking blue at cols 46-47 moved:
- White at cols 50-57 DOWN (shrunk from top)
- White at cols 38-47 UP (expanded up)
This is interesting - it affected TWO white regions! The blue block at cols 46-47 is between white regions at cols 38-47 and 50-57.
Let me think about the pattern:
- Blue at cols 46-47: moves white at cols 50-57 DOWN and white at cols 38-47 UP
This seems to be a "swap" or "exchange" operation. The white regions exchange height.
Let me check the remaining blue block at cols 50-51. But first, let me think about what the goal might be.
Looking at the colored markers now:
- Track 8: charcoal at 29-30, yellow at 31
- Track 5: purple at 39
- Track 2: green at 41
- Track 6: charcoal at 47-48, purple at 49
- Track 7: yellow at 47
- Track 1: charcoal at 51-52, green at 53
Tracks 6 and 7 now have markers at similar heights (47-49 vs 47). Maybe the goal is to align all markers?
Let me try clicking blue at cols 50-51 to see what it does.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Blue at cols 46-47 moved white at cols 50-57 DOWN and white at cols 38-47 UP. It affects two adjacent white regions, exchanging their heights. Tracks 6 and 7 now have markers at similar heights.
Goal model: Possibly align all colored markers at same heights.
Plan: Probe blue at cols 50-51, then look for target configuration.
